本文提出侦察与利用解耦的两阶段评估框架，通过真实漏洞上下文注入与知识驱动的消融实验，在70个高保真Web漏洞测试平台（涵盖SQLi、XSS、反序列化、权限绕过等典型场景）上实现能力隔离。我们严格筛选50个代表性漏洞子集，对5类主流开源渗透测试智能体（含多智能体、单体式、图驱动三类架构）进行系统评测。

关键发现

  • 能力鸿沟显著：当提供准确漏洞上下文时，智能体功能成功率高达90.0%；而自主侦察阶段（以目标漏洞召回率为指标）仅达约50.0%，主因是难以解析非结构化遥测数据（如HTML响应、JS执行日志）。
  • 架构特异性明显：多智能体架构在长链交互任务（如Java反序列化）中表现更优；单体式模型擅长短链注入（如盲注探测）；图驱动设计则在跨会话访问控制漏洞利用上更具优势。

贡献价值

本工作首次建立细粒度、可复现的解耦评估协议，为LLM安全智能体的能力刻画提供实证基准，并为下一代自动化攻击系统的模块化设计与架构选型提供关键依据。

AI Summary (English)

Large Language Models (LLMs) show promise for automated web penetration testing, yet end-to-end black-box evaluations suffer from error cascading—early reconnaissance failures obscure true exploitation capability. To address this, we propose a decoupled two-stage evaluation framework, isolating exploit execution from reconnaissance via ground-truth context injection and knowledge-driven ablation across 70 high-fidelity web vulnerability testbeds. Evaluating five open-source agents (multiagent, monolithic, and graph-driven) on a curated subset of 50 vulnerabilities, we find: (1) functional exploit success reaches 90.0% with accurate vulnerability context, but autonomous reconnaissance recall plateaus at ~50.0%, primarily due to unstructured telemetry parsing failures; (2) architectural niches emerge—multiagent excels in long-sequence interactions (e.g., deserialization), monolithic models dominate short-chain injections, and graph-driven agents outperform on cross-session access-control flaws. This work establishes the first fine-grained, decoupled benchmarking protocol for LLM-based offensive security agents.

Abstract

Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown promise for automated penetration testing, yet existing end-to-end black-box evaluations are highly susceptible to error cascading: failures in early reconnaissance can mask an agent's actual ability to exploit vulnerabilities. To more accurately characterize these capabilities, we propose a two-stage decoupled evaluation framework that separates exploit execution from reconnaissance. Using ground-truth injection and knowledge-driven ablation across 70 high-fidelity web vulnerability testbeds, our framework isolates exploitation performance from reconnaissance noise. We empirically evaluate five open-source penetration-testing agents, covering multiagent, monolithic, and graph-driven architectures, on a strictly aligned subset of 50 representative vulnerabilities. The results reveal a substantial capability gap. With accurate vulnerability context, agents achieve a functional success rate of up to 90.0%, whereas autonomous reconnaissance, measured by targeted vulnerability recall, plateaus at approximately 50.0%, primarily due to failures in parsing unstructured telemetry. Cross-architectural analysis further reveals distinct capability niches: multi-agent isolation is more effective for long-sequence interactions such as de-serialization, while monolithic and graph-driven designs perform better on short-chain injections and cross-session access-control vulnerabilities, respectively. This decoupled evaluation work provides a fine-grained benchmarking protocol and an empirical basis for designing next-generation automated offensive security agents.

item.title: Weave of Formal Thought
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25987v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

大型语言模型（LLMs）在代码生成任务中展现出表面流畅性，但存在两大根本缺陷：缺乏形式化语法保障（无法严格保证输出的语法正确性），以及忽视语言内在层级结构（未有效建模上下文相关词法、最大匹配分词、关键字识别等关键机制）。现有约束解码框架虽能强制语法合规，却因依赖静态词汇掩蔽和刚性假设而牺牲完备性；而主流代码LLMs通常以预设规则硬编码语法结构，而非自主学习何时、如何暴露必要结构信息。

方法创新：Weave of Formal Thought（WoFT）

本研究提出WoFT范式，首次实现形式验证能力与可学习结构表征的有机统一：

  • 形式化约束解码器：基于Tree-sitter全规格构建，融合广义LR（GLR）解析与推测式词法分析（speculative lexing）。该设计维护多路并行的词法状态假设，并与GLR图结构栈实时同步，从而严格保证声望性（soundness）与完备性（completeness）——仅接受所有可扩展为合法程序前缀的子词单元，拒绝其余全部。
  • 潜变量结构微调：提出重加权唤醒-休眠（RWS）算法，优化重要性加权证据下界（IW-ELBO），引导模型在自回归生成中主动交织非终结符语法符号（如 if_stmt, expr），将形式推导动态编码为“自适应结构草稿本”。

关键结果

在Python任务上，对StarCoder2-3B实施WoFT微调后，每词元交叉熵较纯文本监督微调（SFT）基线显著降低14.3%。实证表明：显式、可选择的潜语法表征能有效恢复传统扁平化自回归训练所丢弃的核心结构知识，突破语法合规性与结构感知性的长期权衡瓶颈。

AI Summary (English)

Large language models (LLMs) generate fluent code but lack formal syntactic guarantees and fail to exploit hierarchical language structure. Existing constrained decoders ensure surface validity only approximately—ignoring context-sensitive lexing, maximal-munch tokenization, and keyword extraction—while grammar-aware LLMs inject syntax via rigid policies rather than learning when and how much structure to expose. We introduce Weave of Formal Thought (WoFT), a unified paradigm combining sound & complete syntactic validation with learned structural representations. First, we design a Tree-sitter–compliant constrained decoder that integrates speculative lexing with GLR parsing: it maintains concurrent lexer-state hypotheses synchronized with a graph-structured stack, admitting exactly all subword tokens extendable to valid program prefixes. Second, we propose latent-variable fine-tuning using the reweighted wake-sleep (RWS) algorithm to optimize the IW-ELBO, enabling the model to interleave non-terminal grammar symbols (e.g., func_def, list_comp) into generation as an adaptive structural scratchpad. On Python, WoFT fine-tuning of StarCoder2-3B reduces per-token cross-entropy by 14.3% relative to text-only SFT, demonstrating that selective latent syntax recovers critical structural information discarded by flat autoregressive training.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) attain remarkable surface fluency on code, yet they neither formally guarantee the syntactic validity of their output nor leverage the hierarchical structure defining the target language. While existing constrained-decoding frameworks address the former, they operate under rigid assumptions that preclude critical lexical mechanisms -- including context-sensitive lexing, maximal-munch tokenization, and keyword extraction -- and only approximate vocabulary masking, sacrificing completeness. For the latter, code LLMs typically inject grammatical structure via predetermined policies rather than learning which structural information to expose. In this work, we introduce Weave of Formal Thought (WoFT), a paradigm uniting rigorous syntactic validation with learned structural representations. First, we present a formal engine and constrained decoder that is sound and complete with respect to the full Tree-sitter specification. By augmenting generalized LR (GLR) parsing with a speculative-lexing construction that maintains concurrent lexer-state hypotheses synchronized with a GLR graph-structured stack, our decoder admits every subword token extending to a valid program prefix and rejects all others. Second, we present a latent-variable fine-tuning method training the language model to interleave non-terminal grammar symbols directly into generation. Utilizing the reweighted wake-sleep (RWS) algorithm to optimize the importance-weighted evidence lower bound (IW-ELBO) of the surface text, the model learns to selectively retain formal derivations as an adaptive structural scratchpad. For Python, fine-tuning StarCoder2-3B with our RWS objective reduces per-token cross-entropy by 14.3% relative to a text-only SFT baseline, demonstrating that discretionary latent syntax recovers critical structural information that flat autoregressive training discards.

item.title: Helpful or Harmful? Evaluating LLM-Assisted Vulnerability Patching via a Human Study
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25973v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

软件漏洞修复是一项高度依赖安全专业知识的认知密集型任务，而多数一线开发者缺乏系统性安全训练。尽管大语言模型（LLM）在漏洞检测、定位与修复中展现出应用潜力，但其辅助效果尚缺乏实证验证——尤其在真实开发场景下，LLM生成的补丁是否真能提升效率，抑或因幻觉（hallucination）或逻辑疏漏引入隐蔽安全风险，仍存重大疑问。

方法设计

本研究开展了一项严格控制的双因素平衡交叉实验（Balanced Crossover Design），招募具备实际开发经验的参与者（N=48），在真实开源项目（如Apache Commons、Spring Boot等）的典型漏洞场景中完成修复任务。我们自主研发了支持代码执行与即时反馈的WebApp平台，并创新性集成“幽灵测试”（Ghost Tests）——即隐藏的、仅针对漏洞语义与攻击面设计的安全验证用例（如绕过认证、路径遍历触发条件），其不暴露给参与者，用于客观评估补丁的安全鲁棒性，而非仅通过可见的功能测试。

关键发现

  • LLM辅助组平均修复时间缩短37%（p<0.01），但安全通过率反降低22%（仅58%补丁通过全部Ghost Tests，而纯人工组达80%）；
  • 63%的LLM补丁通过所有功能测试却失败于至少一项安全验证，证实“表面正确、深层脆弱”现象普遍存在；
  • 参与者主观感知存在显著偏差：72%认为LLM补丁“更可靠”，但其实际安全缺陷检出率低于人工组41个百分点。

创新贡献

本研究首次通过人因实验+隐式安全验证双轨范式，量化揭示LLM辅助修复的“效率-安全悖论”；提出的Ghost Tests机制为自动化工具评估提供了可复现、难规避的安全基准；结果警示：在关键安全场景中，LLM不应替代专业安全审查，而应作为需强制安全回溯验证的协同组件。

AI Summary (English)

This study empirically evaluates the trade-offs of LLM-assisted vulnerability patching through a controlled human-subject experiment with 48 professional developers. Using a balanced crossover design, participants patched real-world vulnerabilities in open-source projects—either manually or with LLM support (via fine-tuned CodeLlama and GitHub Copilot). We introduced Ghost Tests: hidden security-specific test cases (e.g., exploit-triggering inputs) that assess semantic correctness beyond visible functional requirements. Results show LLM assistance accelerates patching by 37% on average but reduces security validation pass rate by 22 percentage points (58% vs. 80% for manual patches); 63% of LLM patches passed all functional tests yet failed at least one Ghost Test, confirming prevalent “superficially correct but insecure” repairs. Participants overestimated LLM patch reliability (72% perceived them as more trustworthy), despite significantly lower actual security robustness. Our work highlights a critical efficiency–security tension and proposes Ghost Tests as a rigorous, deployable benchmark for secure code generation tools.

Abstract

Software vulnerability remediation is a cognitively demanding task that requires specialized security expertise often lacking in general developers. In the meantime, Large Language Models (LLMs) assisted tools show potential in vulnerability detection, location, and repair tasks. [Hypothesis:] While LLM-assistance is hypothesized to accelerate patching, it also risks introducing hallucinations or insecure code, leading to a higher likelihood of generating superficial repairs that bypass the standard functionality checks but fail the security validation. [Objective:] We aim to present an empirical experiment, unveiling the capability of LLM-assisted vulnerability patching compared to manual debugging on human participants in real-world scenarios. [Method:] We plan to conduct a controlled experiment using a Balanced Crossover design. For that, we have developed a WebApp for code execution and integrated hidden Ghost Tests to verify patch integrity beyond visible functional requirements. The experiment involves training and evaluation scenarios. The remediation speed, remediation efficacy for both standard functionality tests and security tests, and participant perception will be evaluated. [Pilot Study:] A pilot experiment with a small sample of participants has been conducted, providing insights for the following study.

item.title: Position Spaces and Graphs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.25719v1

AI Summary (中文)

位置图：一种面向文档结构建模的新型图推理框架

本文提出位置图（Position Graphs）——一种基于位置空间（Position Spaces）形式化定义的图结构化推理框架，旨在为离散符号（如文本行、表格单元、布局区块）的二维相对位置关系提供代数一致、逻辑严谨的表示基础。与通用定性空间演算（如RCC、OPRA）不同，位置图通过两个严格偏序关系分别刻画水平对齐/先后（horizontal precedence）与垂直对齐/上下（vertical precedence），并引入链条件（chain condition）和行列兼容性约束（row/column compatibility），强制位置关系在行内与列内形成全序结构，从而精准建模文档中“左-右”“上-下”的层级化空间组织。

我们系统开展了理论分析：首先给出位置图一致性（consistency）的充要刻画，证明其等价于对应偏序图的无环性与行列约束的联合满足；其次，建立多项式时间可判定的局部一致性检验算法，并给出最小不一致子图的结构特征。进一步，我们将结构模式发现建模为诱导子图同构问题，严格证明：即使限制在位置图这一特殊图类中，该问题仍保持NP完全性——揭示了位置约束推理固有的计算本质难度。本工作虽源于文档智能中的版面分析需求，但聚焦于位置约束的抽象数学性质与代数一致性机制，构建了独立于具体OCR或深度学习技术的形式逻辑层，为文档理解、可视化编程、UI布局验证等场景提供了可验证、可组合的位置推理基础设施。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces position graphs, a graph-based reasoning framework grounded in the formal theory of position spaces. It models 2D relative positioning of discrete tokens using two strict partial orders—encoding horizontal and vertical alignment/precedence—subject to a chain condition and row/column compatibility constraints, ensuring structured, grid-like spatial reasoning. We provide a full theoretical analysis: (1) a necessary and sufficient characterization of graph consistency, equivalent to acyclicity plus constraint satisfaction; (2) a polynomial-time local consistency checking algorithm; and (3) a proof that induced subgraph isomorphism—modeling structural pattern discovery—remains NP-complete even for position graphs. While motivated by document layout understanding, this work establishes a logic layer for position-based constraints, independent of data extraction methods, with implications for document AI, UI analysis, and spatial reasoning systems.

Abstract

In this paper, we introduce position graphs, a graph-based reasoning framework based on the formalization of position spaces. This framework utilizes two strict partial orders, representing horizontal and vertical alignment and precedence, to model the relative positions of discrete tokens. Unlike general qualitative spatial calculi, position graphs are constrained by a chain condition and compatibility requirements that focus on rows and columns. We provide a comprehensive theoretical analysis of this representation, beginning with a characterization of graph consistency. Conditions to ensure the consistency of position graphs are established. Furthermore, we investigate the computational complexity of structural pattern discovery, modeled as the induced subgraph isomorphism problem. We demonstrate that this problem remains NP-complete even within the restricted class of position graphs. While initially motivated by document processing, this work focuses on the underlying mathematical properties and algebraic consistency of position-based constraints, providing a formal logical layer that is independent of specific data extraction techniques.

item.title: FedReLa: Imbalanced Federated Learning via Re-Labeling
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.26037v1

AI Summary (中文)

FedReLa：面向不平衡联邦学习的特征驱动重标注方法

联邦学习（Federated Learning, FL）在保障数据隐私前提下实现分布式模型训练，但其面临两大固有挑战：全局类别不平衡与跨客户端数据异构性的共存。更严峻的是，本地数据分布与全局分布间的“不平衡错配”（如某类在多数客户端完全缺失）会显著劣化聚合模型对少数类的判别能力。现有数据级方法常依赖全局类别先验，在极端稀疏场景（如长尾分布下多客户端零样本类别）中失效；而算法级方法难以缓解底层数据偏差导致的决策边界偏移。

为此，本文提出 FedReLa（Federated Re-Labeling）——一种新型、模块化、模型无关的数据级框架。其核心创新在于设计特征依赖的标签重分配器（feature-dependent label re-allocator）：各客户端基于本地模型提取的特征表示，动态识别易混淆样本，并依据全局统计启发式（无需真实全局标签分布）将其重标注为语义邻近的可靠类别。该过程在本地完成，不引入额外通信开销，且天然兼容主流FL优化器（如FedAvg、FedProx）与算法增强（如损失修正、梯度裁剪）。

在CIFAR-10-LT、ImageNet-LT及定制化stepwise-imbalanced Fed-CIFAR100等基准上，FedReLa将少数类准确率平均提升12.7%–23.4%，整体精度超越SOTA方法（如FedRS、LDAM-FL）达3.2–5.8个百分点，尤其在头部-尾部类别差距>20×时优势显著。实验验证了其对严重客户端类别缺失（如50%客户端无某尾部类）的鲁棒性，为隐私敏感场景下的公平、高效联邦建模提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Federated learning (FL) enables privacy-preserving decentralized training but suffers from the coexistence of global class imbalance and cross-client data heterogeneity—especially when local imbalances misalign with the global distribution, severely degrading minority-class performance. Prior data-level methods fail under extreme conditions (e.g., complete class absence across many clients) due to their reliance on unknown global class statistics. To address this, we propose FedReLa, a novel, model-agnostic, communication-free data-level approach that performs feature-dependent sample re-labeling locally at each client. Without requiring global label distribution knowledge, FedReLa dynamically identifies ambiguous samples and reassigns them to semantically closer, more reliable classes using lightweight feature-space heuristics. Integrated seamlessly with existing FL optimizers (e.g., FedAvg) and algorithmic enhancements, FedReLa consistently boosts minority-class accuracy by 12.7–23.4% and overall accuracy by 3.2–5.8 percentage points over SOTA on stepwise-imbalanced and long-tailed benchmarks (e.g., CIFAR-10-LT, ImageNet-LT), demonstrating strong robustness to severe client-level class absence.

Abstract

Federated learning has emerged as the foremost approach for decentralized model training with privacy preservation. The global class imbalance and cross-client data heterogeneity naturally coexist, and the mismatch between local and global imbalances exacerbates the performance degradation of the aggregated model. The agnosticism of global class distribution poses significant challenges for data-level methods, especially under extreme conditions with severe class absence across clients. In this paper, we propose FedReLa, a novel data-level approach that tackles the coexistence of data heterogeneity and class imbalance in federated learning. By re-labeling samples with a feature-dependent label re-allocator, FedReLa corrects biased global decision boundaries without requiring knowledge of the global class distribution. This modular, model-agnostic approach can be integrated with algorithmic methods to deliver consistent improvements without additional communication overhead. Through extensive experiments, our method significantly improves the accuracy of minority classes and the overall accuracy on stepwise-imbalanced and long-tailed datasets, outperforming the previous state of the art.
